column3: Description
column4: Precondition

column2: Verify successful login with valid email and password
column3: Verify successful login when user enters valid email and password
column4: User is on the login page.
column5: 1. User enters valid email address. 2. User enters valid password. 3. User clicks "Login" button.
column6: User is redirected to the VWO dashboard.

column2: Validate email format validation
column3: Verify email format validation on submission of the email field
column4: User is on the login page with an email field.
column5: 1. Enter invalid email address (e.g., missing @ symbol). 2. Submit the form.
column6: The form should display an error message indicating invalid email format.

column2: Test login with default password
column3: Test login when the user enters a default password
column4: User is on the login page.
column5: 1. Enter a default password. 2. Click "Login" button.
column6: User is successfully logged in to the VWO dashboard.

column2: Verify 2FA functionality (optional)
column3: Test the 2FA setup process for new users
column4: User is on the login page.
column5: 1. User clicks “Forgot Password” link. 2. User enters valid email address. 3. User clicks “Reset Password” link.
column6: 2FA should be initiated for the user. 4. User is redirected to a 2FA confirmation page.

column2: Check login form responsiveness on mobile devices
column3: Test login form on a mobile device with a small screen
column4: User is on the login page.
column5: 1. Resize the browser to a mobile screen.
column6: The login form should automatically adjust to a smaller screen size, maintaining readability and touch-friendly elements.

column2: Verify password reset process flow
column3: Test the password reset flow for a valid email address
column4: User is on the login page.
column5: 1. User enters a valid email address. 2. User clicks "Reset Password" button.
column6: User receives an email with a reset password link. 3. User clicks the link. 4. User enters a new password. 5. User confirms the new password. 6. User is redirected to the VWO dashboard.

column2: Confirm successful login with limited data
column3: Test login with email and password
column4: User is on the login page.
column5: 1. Enter valid email and password. 2. Click Login
column6: User is redirected to the VWO dashboard.

column2: Test account recovery flow with email verification
column3: Verify email verification process for account recovery
column4: User is on the login page.
column5: 1. User enters recovery email. 2. User clicks "Verify Email."
column6: Email is sent to the user’s email address. 3. User clicks a verification link. 4. User is redirected to the VWO dashboard.

column2: Assess login speed and loading times on a slow network
column3: Measure login time with low network connectivity
column4: User is on the login page.
column5: 1. Simulate a slow network connection. 2. Observe login time.
column6: Login time should decrease within a reasonable timeframe (e.g., 30-60 seconds).

column2: Check for broken links or error messages
column3: Verify that an error message appears if a field is invalid
column4: User is on the login page with a malformed email field.
column5: 1. Enter a malformed email address. 2. Submit form.
column6: Error message should be displayed indicating the field is invalid.

column2: Test SSO integration with SAML protocol
column3: Verify SSO login with SAML authentication
column4: User is on the login page.
column5: 1. Login with SAML credentials. 2. Verify that the user is redirected to the VWO dashboard.
column6: Successful SSO authentication is completed.

column2: Validate accessibility features on mobile devices
column3: Check that all accessibility features are correctly rendered on a mobile screen
column4: User is on the login page.
column5: 1. Resize the browser to a mobile screen. 2. Validate accessibility settings.
column6: All accessibility features (e.g., keyboard navigation, ARIA labels) should be correctly rendered on a mobile device.

column2: Verify success during login with additional data
column3: Test login with a valid user data profile
column4: User is on the login page.
column5: 1. Enter a valid user profile data. 2. Click Login
column6: Successful login with appropriate authentication permissions.

column2: Assess login performance with higher user load
column3: Test login performance with a surge of users
column4: User is on the login page.
column5: 1. Simulate a high number of users logging in simultaneously.
column6: Login should complete within a reasonable time (e.g., 10 seconds).
